entities.name: JATROPHA
entities.description: A plant species (Jatropha curcas) studied for its physiological adaptation and root growth in response to drought stress.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain function and survive under water-limited conditions.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by insufficient water availability for the plant.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: The biological process by which a plant adjusts its functions to survive environmental stressors like drought.

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The development and expansion of the root system, including length, weight, and distribution.

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DENSITY
entities.description: A measure of the total length of roots per unit volume of soil.

entities.name: ROOT DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The mass of the root system after the removal of water content.

entities.name: BIOMASS ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The increase in organic matter and mass within the root system.

entities.name: RESOURCE ALLOCATION
entities.description: The distribution of energy and nutrients to the roots relative to other plant organs.

entities.name: TAP ROOT
entities.description: The primary, central root that grows vertically downward.

entities.name: LATERAL ROOTS
entities.description: Secondary roots that branch out horizontally from the tap root.

entities.name: ROOTING DEPTH
entities.description: The maximum vertical distance the root system penetrates into the soil.

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The spatial arrangement and density of root length across different soil layers.

entities.name: ROOT TO PLANT RATIO
entities.description: The proportion of root biomass compared to the total biomass of the plant.

entities.name: ROOT BRANCHING
entities.description: The pattern and frequency of lateral root formation from the main root axis.

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of topsoil, which can be mitigated by plant root systems.

entities.name: DROUGHT RESPONSE
entities.description: The set of physiological and morphological reactions a plant exhibits when exposed to drought.

entities.name: DROUGHT AVOIDANCE
entities.description: A survival strategy where a plant avoids drought stress through mechanisms like dormancy or leaf shedding.

entities.name: DORMANT STATE
entities.description: A period of suspended growth and reduced metabolic activity to survive adverse conditions.

entities.name: LEAF DROP
entities.description: The shedding of leaves to reduce water loss through transpiration during drought stress.
